q: A surface associated with a propagating wave which is a locus of all points in the wave that have the same phase is a ___.
On a particular wavefront, at a given moment of time, all particles of the medium are undergoing the same phase.
Wavefront is always ___ to the direction of propagation (or) rays.
How many types of wavefront are there?
A plane wavefront transforms into a spherical wavefront when it is far away from the source.
The angular position of a point of a periodic wave is known as the ___.
The different states of the periodic waves at the same instant is represented by ___ difference.
If the phase difference is $2\pi, 4\pi, 6\pi, \dots$, these two positions are in ___.
If phase difference is $\pi, 3\pi, 5\pi, \dots$, the positions are completely out of phase.
The distance between two points measured along the direction of propagation of the wave is the ___ difference.
If path difference is $0, \lambda, 2\lambda, 3\lambda, \dots$, it is said the waves are in ___.
If path difference is $\frac{\lambda}{2}, \frac{3\lambda}{2}, \frac{5\lambda}{2}, \dots$, the waves are completely out of phase.
The relation between phase difference and path difference is $\Delta\phi = $ ___.
Young was able to explain his experiment result as an ___ phenomenon.
When a plane wave passes through a slit, what type of wavelet emerges?
Waves emitted from two slits in Young's experiment have different frequencies.
Two waves are coherent if they have the same frequency, same amplitude and are in ___.
The interference pattern consists of a series of bright and dark parallel lines called ___ .
Constructive interference occurs at the centre of the screen because the waves travel the ___ distance.
The bright fringe at the centre of the screen is called the central bright line.
If path difference is an integral number of wavelengths ($0, \lambda, 2\lambda, \dots$), the waves interfere ___.
Constructive interference results in ___ fringes.
If path difference is a half-integral number of wavelengths, the waves interfere ___.
Destructive interference results in a dark fringe.
The position of bright and dark fringes on the screen is denoted by ___ .
For a bright fringe, the path difference $d \sin \theta$ is equal to ___.
In the formula for bright fringes, $m$ is called the order of bright fringe.
The zeroth order bright fringe corresponds to the central bright line at $\theta = $ ___.
For dark fringes, the path difference $d \sin \theta$ is equal to ___ .
The distance between two adjacent bright (or dark) fringes is called the ___.
Fringe width $\Delta y$ is expressed as $\frac{\lambda D}{d}$.
The position of fringes and fringe width depend on the ___ of the light source.
Bright and dark fringes are formed alternatively where the bright fringe has ___ intensity.
The intensity of the dark fringe is zero.
Monochromatic light means that it has a ___ wavelength.
